title: Business Analyst fur die Entwicklung integrierter Wertpapierpartnerprozesse
required_skills: Experience in maintaining and optimising existing products
Ability to develop and align concepts with stakeholders on complex requirements
Customer advisory and long-term client relationship skills
matched_skills: Degree (Master of Management & Entrepreneurship, Leuphana) — satisfies education requirement
4+ years of professional experience across multiple roles — satisfies 2-3 years experience requirement
Stakeholder concept alignment: coordinated deliverables across agile sprints at Hapag-Lloyd and managed 6 workstreams at HSBC — satisfies concept alignment with stakeholders requirement
Analytical skills: KPI analysis, budget variance dashboards, data storytelling at HSBC — satisfies analytical/problem-solving requirement
Communication and presentation: sprint review reports, C-suite escalation narratives, PowerPoint production — satisfies communication/presentation requirement
JIRA and Confluence usage at Hapag-Lloyd — partial IT project context match
missing_skills: No securities/capital markets (Wertpapiergeschaeft) domain knowledge — this is the core business context of the role
No banking IT or Sparkassen ecosystem experience
No product ownership or 2nd-level support experience
No test planning or test automation experience
No IT development lifecycle experience (software delivery, requirements engineering for IT systems)
core_responsibilities: Participate in IT projects for Sparkassen and partners (securities business focus)
Develop and align concepts with stakeholders for complex requirements
Maintain and optimise existing applications and products in the securities business
Assume product responsibility for applications including customer advisory in 2nd-level support
Plan and execute tests, including first steps towards test automation
Conduct detailed data analyses to support decisions and present results
Analyse market trends and derive recommendations to improve customer journey
recommended_emphasis: Highlight agile sprint coordination at Hapag-Lloyd and the cross-stakeholder concept alignment experience as closest match to requirements development and alignment
Emphasise the analytical reporting and data storytelling work at HSBC as evidence for data analysis and decision support
Acknowledge the securities domain gap openly if applying — position banking (HSBC FinTech) experience as adjacent sector familiarity
Do not claim IT product ownership experience — this is a genuine gap that cannot be stretched from the CV
summary: Weak match at 46.2%. The candidate satisfies the generic requirements (degree, analytical skills, stakeholder coordination, communication) but lacks the critical domain expertise in securities/capital markets (Wertpapiergeschaeft) and banking IT product management that define this role. The Sparkassen IT context is highly specialised. Marginal application candidate — only advisable if the candidate is open to a significant domain pivot and can articulate HSBC FinTech experience as a bridge.
